design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore overworld/dungeons → encounter random battles → gain experience and gil → upgrade equipment → progress through story bosses → restore elemental crystals
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource attrition (HP/MP management) versus progression necessity; party vulnerability versus enemy encounters requiring grinding
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead four elemental warriors on a quest to restore balance to the world by defeating four Fiends and restoring darkened crystals
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Locked party composition at start; random encounter design that demands grinding for progression; turn-based menu-driven combat as foundational system; class-based character progression with upgrade paths; spatial exploration as primary pacing mechanism

difficulty.lives_system: Party total death = game over; no individual character permadeath, but requires reload from last save
difficulty.checkpointing: Manual save only; no checkpoints within dungeons
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can grind random encounters to raise levels and stats; can choose party composition at start; can manage resources (items, magic) tactically during battles; equipment choices affect survivability

progression.upgrade_system: Experience-based leveling (1-50); class promotion available mid-game granting sprite evolution and new ability access; equipment purchases from shops using gil; magic learned via leveling or class
progression.unlock_mechanism: Dungeon/town progression unlocks new areas; vehicles (ship, canoe, airship) unlock based on story progression; class upgrades available at story checkpoint
replayability_hooks: Four character class choices at start create different party compositions
Class upgrade timing and benefits vary by class selection
Multiple vehicle types enable different traversal strategies
Treasure chest hunting in dungeons incentivizes exploration

world_building_techniques: Environmental design (forests, caves, mountains, underwater caverns, swamps, buildings) each suggest distinct regions
NPC dialogue in towns provides exposition and hints for progression
Dungeon placement and naming establish world geography
Elemental theming (four Fiends, four crystals) creates thematic coherence
Shop and item inventories suggest economic and cultural context

boss_design.philosophy: Four Elemental Fiends serve as major story beats; each represents darkening of a crystal element; preset locations establish encounters as story-critical
notable_bosses: Three additional Elemental Fiends (not detailed in sources)

faction_indicators: Elemental alignment (Fire, Water, Earth, Wind Fiends suggest opposing cosmic forces)
Warriors of Light (player party) versus Fiends (darkness forces)
danger_signals: Random encounter frequency suggests hostile world state
Dungeon presence in populated areas signals active threats
environmental_storytelling: Darkened crystals visible in world as corrupted locations
Town placement suggests settlements near protective or resource sites
Dungeon architecture indicates ancient structures or current threats
Underwater and swamp locations suggest elemental influence zones
